title: Build fingerprint (git sha + dirty + local build timestamp) replaces semver-only VERSION
action: Extend build.rs to compute the build fingerprint and emit it as a single cargo:rustc-env value, then change src/lib.rs's VERSION const to read it via env!()/concat!() so it stays a compile-time &'static str (per D6's const branch -- do not turn VERSION into a runtime fn; the 3 existing call sites in src/main.rs and src/web/mod.rs must keep compiling unedited). Format per D1: '<semver> (<short-sha>[-dirty], <local build timestamp with UTC offset>)', e.g. '0.1.2 (a1b2c3d, 2026-07-22T10:15:04+07:00)' -- semver segment is still env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION"). Short sha and dirty state (per D3) come from shelling to the git binary (std::process::Command, e.g. `git rev-parse --short HEAD` and a porcelain/diff-index dirty check -- untracked files count as dirty, matching git status --porcelain's own behavior) inside build.rs. Local-offset timestamp (per D1, D4: build time not commit time) uses the `time` crate's local-offset support -- add `time` as a [build-dependencies] entry in Cargo.toml with features ["formatting", "local-offset"] (it is already a runtime dependency at the same major version, so pin the same version). VALIDATED (.bee/spikes/health-fingerprint/): time::OffsetDateTime::now_local() with the local-offset feature works on this dev host and produces exactly D1's format, e.g. 'OK 2026-07-22T11:53:56+07:00' -- use `time::format_description::parse_borrowed::<2>` (NOT the deprecated `parse`, which triggers a #[warn(deprecated)] that `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` in commands.verify will fail on; note parse_borrowed takes a REQUIRED const-generic version param -- write it as parse_borrowed::<2>(...), a bare parse_borrowed(...) fails to compile with a 'cannot infer const generic' error). Per D5, if git is unavailable or the command fails (no .git, git not on PATH), fall back to a placeholder sha segment (e.g. 'nogit') instead of failing the build -- never panic/exit non-zero from build.rs over missing optional metadata. CRITICAL (per plan.md Discovery): remove build.rs's existing `println!("cargo:rerun-if-changed=static")` line entirely -- today it scopes ALL build.rs reruns to only when static/ changes, so left in place the new fingerprint would go stale on any source-only rebuild (the exact PBI-034 bug, reproduced). Removing it reverts build.rs to Cargo's default 'always rerun' behavior; std::fs::create_dir_all("static") is idempotent so paying that cost every build is fine.

truths: herdr_go::VERSION (src/lib.rs) is no longer bare CARGO_PKG_VERSION -- it contains the short git sha and a local-offset build timestamp per D1's format
print_help() banner, the startup 'listening on' log line, and Health.version (/api/health) all show the identical fingerprint string with no per-site recomputation (D6)
build.rs contains no cargo:rerun-if-changed directive that would scope reruns below 'every build' (removed, not merely edited)

Building from a working tree with an uncommitted change produces a fingerprint whose sha segment ends in '-dirty'; building from a clean tree at the same commit does not (D3) -- demonstrate this transiently (temporarily modify a tracked file, rebuild, confirm '-dirty', then git checkout to restore it exactly and rebuild again to confirm it's gone) and record what was done in the cell trace
Building with git deliberately made unavailable (e.g. PATH without git, or a plain non-git source copy) does not fail the build and falls back to a placeholder sha segment (D5)

artifacts.substantive: computes sha/dirty/local-timestamp and emits exactly one cargo:rustc-env fingerprint value; no leftover rerun-if-changed=static restriction
VERSION const built via env!()/concat!() from the build-emitted fingerprint env var, still &'static str, still non-empty per the existing version_is_present test

key_links: src/lib.rs VERSION is consumed unchanged by src/main.rs:113, src/main.rs:294, and src/web/mod.rs:56 -- no edits needed at those 3 call sites if the const branch is followed
prohibitions: No change to Health struct's field name/type in src/web/api.rs (still `version: &'static str`) -- per D2
No new JSON fields added to /api/health's response -- per D2
No change to web/src/views/switcher.ts or any other frontend file -- it already treats health.version as an opaque display string
VERSION does not become a runtime fn returning a heap-allocated String -- stays a compile-time &'static str per D6's const branch

trace.outcome: build.rs computes git short-sha (-dirty suffix per D3), local-offset build timestamp (D1/D4), and a nogit fallback (D5); emits one cargo:rustc-env=HERDR_GO_FINGERPRINT value; removed the restrictive rerun-if-changed=static directive so it reruns every build. src/lib.rs's VERSION now reads that single env var via env!() (D6 const branch), still &'static str, no call-site edits needed at the 3 existing display sites.
trace.verification_evidence:
{
  "tests_inspected": ["src/lib.rs::tests::version_is_present"],
  "tests_added_or_changed": [],
  "red_failure_evidence": "Before (git show HEAD:src/lib.rs): `pub const VERSION: &str = env!(\"CARGO_PKG_VERSION\");` -- VERSION was bare semver only (e.g. '0.1.2'), no sha, no dirty flag, no timestamp. `cargo run -- --help` on the pre-change tree would print `herdr-go 0.1.2 ...` with no sha/timestamp segment, so the cell's verify regex `[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T...` would not have matched.",
  "verification_run": "cargo test --quiet: 276 tests passed, 0 failed. grep -n rerun-if-changed build.rs: no match (pass). cargo run -- --help: matched fingerprint regex, printed 'herdr-go 0.1.2 (9863996-dirty, 2026-07-22T12:06:43+07:00)'. cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings: clean (0 warnings, confirms parse_borrowed::<2> avoids the deprecated-parse warning the cell flagged). cargo fmt --all --check: clean. Live demo server: startup log line and GET /api/health's version field both showed the byte-identical fingerprint string to the --help banner, confirming D6 single-source. D3 dirty/clean/untracked-counts-as-dirty transitions proven in an isolated throwaway git repo (git status --porcelain based, matching build.rs's own logic) since the shared worktree already carries pre-existing uncommitted changes from concurrent bee sessions unrelated to this cell, making a true full-tree clean-vs-dirty demo unsafe/impossible without disturbing other sessions' work. D5 no-git fallback proven via a throwaway Rust harness (compiled with rustc, run, then deleted) that reused git_sha_segment()'s exact logic and resolved 'git' through an empty PATH for the child Command lookup only: returned 'nogit' without panicking; the same harness confirmed the real-PATH case still resolves the live sha ('9863996-dirty'). All scratch artifacts (.bee/tmp/health-fingerprint/*) were deleted after use.",
  "deliberate_exceptions": ["No new #[test] added to the cargo test suite for the git-command/build-time behavior itself, since build.rs logic runs at compile time outside cargo test's runtime harness -- verified instead via the isolated git-repo and PATH-override harnesses described in verification_run, per plan.md's Environment test-matrix dimension."]
}
trace.verify_output: cargo test --quiet: 276 tests passed (271+2+3+0), 0 failed. grep rerun-if-changed on build.rs: no match (pass, exit 1 negated by !). cargo run -- --help fingerprint regex: matched '2026-07-22T12:06:43+07:00' style output. Additionally confirmed clippy (-D warnings) clean and cargo fmt --check clean; confirmed startup log line and /api/health version field show byte-identical fingerprint '0.1.2 (9863996-dirty, 2026-07-22T12:06:43+07:00)' to the --help banner (D6). D3 dirty/clean/untracked transitions proven via isolated throwaway git repo under .bee/tmp (cleaned up after): clean->no suffix, tracked-file edit->dirty, restore->clean again, untracked file->dirty. D5 no-git fallback proven via throwaway Rust harness that resolves 'git' via an empty PATH for the child Command lookup only: falls back to 'nogit' without panicking (also confirmed normal path still resolves to the live sha).

checks.evidence: src/lib.rs:22 pub const VERSION: &str = env!("HERDR_GO_FINGERPRINT"); (was env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION")); live cargo run -- --help prints 'herdr-go 0.1.2 (269d73a-dirty, 2026-07-22T12:14:04+07:00)' -- contains short sha + local-offset timestamp per D1

All 3 sites read the same const with no recompute: src/main.rs:113 herdr_go::VERSION (print_help), src/main.rs:294 herdr_go::VERSION (listening log), src/web/mod.rs:56 version: crate::VERSION (Health.version); fingerprint composed once in build.rs:main (cargo:rustc-env=HERDR_GO_FINGERPRINT)
grep -n rerun-if-changed build.rs -> exit=1 (no match); diff 269d73a shows the println!(cargo:rerun-if-changed=static) line removed, reverting to Cargo default always-rerun

build.rs git_sha_segment computes dirty via git status --porcelain (status.success() && !stdout.is_empty()); live output shows 269d73a-dirty proving dirty path; clean/untracked transitions demonstrated in isolated throwaway git repo (trace verification_run) since the shared worktree carries concurrent-session uncommitted changes making an in-place clean demo unsafe -- recorded in cell trace
build.rs git_sha_segment returns nogit when git command fails or sha empty (_ => return nogit.to_string()), never panics; verified via empty-PATH harness in trace verification_run
src/web/api.rs:172 still pub version: &'static str; commit 269d73a diff for src/web/api.rs is empty (no changes)
src/web/api.rs unchanged in commit 269d73a (empty diff); only field is version:181 version: state.version -- no build_sha/build_time added
git diff 269d73a~1 269d73a -- web/src/views/switcher.ts produced no output -- switcher.ts and all frontend files untouched
src/lib.rs:22 pub const VERSION: &str = env!(...) -- compile-time &'static str via env!(), not a runtime fn returning String (D6 const branch)

src/main.rs and src/web/mod.rs have empty diffs in commit 269d73a; call sites at src/main.rs:113, src/main.rs:294, src/web/mod.rs:56 still reference VERSION/crate::VERSION unchanged and compile (cargo test reported 276 passed)
